## Frequently asked questions

[What is an envelope?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-bc2283069d90d9ccea77)

An envelope is the process of sending a document to one or more recipients for electronic signing. It involves preparing the document by adding fillable fields and then sending the document to recipients to sign electronically.

[Who is a "sender"?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-8417e7169d90d9ccea77)

A “sender” is anyone who has a BoldSign account and can send documents out for signature. Anyone that just receives a document for signing is classified as a “recipient” and does not need a BoldSign account or a license.

[How do I estimate my cost?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ddac1bc69d90d9ccea77)

Use the calculator link.

[Will my signers need to pay or need an account for signing?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3dad4e069d90d9ccea77)

No, your signers do not need to pay or register with us to sign the documents that you send.

[Will my subscriptions be automatically renewed?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ecc498569d90d9ccea77)

Yes, your subscriptions will be automatically renewed at the end of the billing cycle. However, you can easily cancel anytime by logging into your account.

[Are there any limitations during the free 30-day trial?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-af2712069d90d9ccea77)

All trial accounts will have access to the enterprise-level features, so everyone will be able to evaluate all the available features. However, there are some usage restrictions that apply:

- Maximum number of senders is restricted to 25.
- Maximum number of documents that can be signed is restricted to 250.
- Maximum number of documents that can be signed using bulk links is restricted to 250.
- Maximum number of API documents is restricted to 250.

[Are BoldSign’s eSignatures legally compliant?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3b895a169d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign is legally compliant with popular U.S. (ESIGN) and international (eIDAS) eSignature laws. We record all the major activity that happens during the signing process and provide a downloadable copy of the complete audit trail report. The final document is also digitally signed so that any potential tampering in the future can be immediately detected.

[Does BoldSign offer special pricing for non-profit organizations?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-7227f4869d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign provides a specially designed eSignature solution for non-profit organizations. Our Growth Plan is completely free, and we provide a 50% discount for Business and Premium plans. Discover more on our [non-profit page](https://boldsign.com/solutions/electronic-signature-for-non-profit-organizations/ "https://boldsign.com/solutions/electronic-signature-for-non-profit-organizations/").

[What is the Pay As You Go option in BoldSign?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-5d0c2eb69d90d9ccea77)

Our [Pay-As-You-Go](https://support.boldsign.com/kb/article/21241/pay-as-you-go-for-boldsign) (PAYG) model works with Business plans and above. You get all core features in your subscription, and PAYG applies only when you exceed your limits or use add-ons like SMS delivery, WhatsApp notifications, bulk sending, identity verification, and more.

[Is there a free way to request an e-signature?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-07b7de469d90d9ccea77)

Yes, BoldSign offers a free Essential plan that allows individuals and micro-businesses to request up to 25 envelopes per month with no cost, including features like audit trails, signer authentication and automated reminders.

[How much does BoldSign cost?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-68e27d969d90d9ccea77)

BoldSign offers a free plan(Essential), paid plans start at $5/month(Growth), Business and Premium include advanced features for teams, and Enterprise plans with API access start at $30/month for additional enterprise-level capabilities.

[What is an envelope?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-bc2283069d90d9ce54c0)

An envelope is the process of sending a document to one or more recipients for electronic signing. It involves preparing the document by adding fillable fields and then sending the document to recipients to sign electronically.

[Who is a user?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-8417e7169d90d9ce54c0)

In the API plan, a user is anyone responsible for configuring the BoldSign account for integration purposes. This includes tasks like generating API keys, creating OAuth apps, monitoring the developer console, and managing webhook configurations.

[How does API pricing work?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3dad4e069d90d9ce54c0)

**Free Sandbox:** This plan is for testing our APIs. You can integrate our APIs into your application for free. However, the documents created will be watermarked as “This is a test document” and automatically deleted after 14 days.

**Enterprise API:** The Enterprise API pricing starts from $30/month, includes 40 envelopes. Once you’ve used the 40 envelopes, a pay-as-you-go plan will get started, costing $0.75 per envelope.

**Custom Plan:** We also provide customized pricing options to fit your unique needs.

Please refer to this link for more information about API pricing: [BoldSign API Pricing](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=api)

[How are envelopes counted?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-ecc498569d90d9ce54c0)

You will be charged per envelope sent for signature. You can add multiple documents within a single envelope, and they will be considered as one envelope.

[Can I test BoldSign's API?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-af2712069d90d9ce54c0)

Yes, you can test BoldSign’s API with the free Sandbox plan. Remember, documents created with this plan will be watermarked, and deleted after 14 days.

[Will my documents be secure?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-3b895a169d90d9ce54c0)

Yes, documents created with BoldSign are entirely secure. Your data is protected so that even our staff cannot access it for troubleshooting purposes. Please refer to this link for more information: [BoldSign security policy](https://boldsign.com/security-policy/).

[Which plan is best for me and my team?](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-pricing/?plan=webapp#collapse-091cb6869d90d9ce54c0)

Check out [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cj5SWTOJdWM) to compare our different paid plans and select the one that best fits your needs.

# is boldsign cheaper than docusign

![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

Shunfang


![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

2025-12-01


![](https://static.esignglobal.com/6deccd6f_f421_5f11_ac14_52f10c173fee_8_19_3fc66c6c73.png)

3min


![Twitter](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/twitter.svg)![Facebook](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/facebook.svg)![Linkedin](https://static.esignglobal.com/publish-assets/images/news/link.svg)

## Comparing BoldSign and DocuSign: A Cost Analysis for Businesses

In the competitive landscape of electronic signature solutions, businesses often weigh options like BoldSign and DocuSign based on pricing, features, and scalability. As a commercial observer, this article dives into whether BoldSign offers a cheaper alternative to DocuSign, drawing from verified 2025 pricing data and market insights. We'll explore their core plans, add-ons, and value propositions to help decision-makers evaluate total cost of ownership.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251022_1636_simple_compose_01k85gx9g0fxybwyekzqatj7vw_98c55ccdc2.png)

## Is BoldSign Cheaper Than DocuSign? Breaking Down the Pricing Models

### Understanding BoldSign's Pricing Structure

BoldSign, developed by Zoho Corporation, positions itself as an affordable, user-friendly e-signature tool aimed at small to medium-sized businesses (SMBs). Its pricing is straightforward and subscription-based, with no hidden envelope limits in higher tiers, making it appealing for cost-conscious teams.

- **Free Plan**: Completely free for up to 3 documents per month, including basic signing, templates, and audit trails. Ideal for freelancers or very low-volume users.
- **Essential Plan**: $10 per month (billed annually) for unlimited users and up to 50 documents per month. Features include custom branding, API access, and multi-language support.
- **Comprehensive Plan**: $15 per month (annual billing) with unlimited documents, advanced workflows like conditional routing, and integrations with Zoho apps or third-party tools like Google Workspace.
- **Enterprise Plan**: Custom pricing starting around $25 per user per month, adding SSO, advanced reporting, and compliance tools for larger organizations.

BoldSign's model emphasizes unlimited users across plans, which reduces costs for teams. Add-ons like SMS delivery or identity verification are usage-based, typically $0.10–$0.50 per action, but rarely exceed 20% of base costs for moderate use. In regions like the US or EU, where e-signature laws such as ESIGN Act or eIDAS mandate basic electronic validity, BoldSign complies without extra fees, keeping it economical.

### DocuSign's Pricing: A Tiered Approach with Envelope Limits

DocuSign, a market leader since 2004, offers robust enterprise-grade features but at a premium. Its 2025 pricing (US region, annual billing) is seat-based with strict envelope quotas, which can inflate costs for high-volume users. From official sources:

- **Personal Plan**: $120/year ($10/month) for 1 user and 5 envelopes/month. Basic signing and templates, but limited scalability.
- **Standard Plan**: $300/user/year ($25/month/user), up to 50 users, ~100 envelopes/user/year. Adds team collaboration, comments, and reminders.
- **Business Pro Plan**: $480/user/year ($40/month/user), similar envelope limits but includes web forms, conditional logic, bulk send, and payment collection.
- **Enterprise/Advanced**: Custom, often $50+/user/month, with SSO, governance, and unlimited envelopes for high compliance needs.

Add-ons like Identity Verification (IDV) or SMS delivery are metered: IDV at $1–$5 per verification, SMS at $0.20–$0.50 per message. API plans start at $600/year for 40 envelopes/month, escalating to $5,760/year for advanced features. Automation sends (e.g., bulk or API) are capped at ~10/month/user, even in higher tiers, potentially requiring upgrades.

In APAC regions like China or Singapore, DocuSign faces challenges: cross-border latency, data residency surcharges, and limited local ID methods can add 20–50% to costs. For instance, Singapore's Electronic Transactions Act requires secure, auditable signatures, which DocuSign supports but at higher effective pricing due to regional compliance add-ons.

### Direct Price Comparison: BoldSign vs. DocuSign

To answer the core question—is BoldSign cheaper than DocuSign?—let's compare equivalent scenarios for a 5-user team sending 200 envelopes/year.

- **Low-Volume (50 envelopes/year)**: BoldSign Essential ($10/month total, $600/year for team) vs. DocuSign Personal/Standard ($1,500/year for 5 users at $25/month each). BoldSign saves ~60%, with unlimited users and no per-envelope fees.
- **Medium-Volume (200 envelopes/year)**: BoldSign Comprehensive ($15/month total, $900/year) covers unlimited envelopes vs. DocuSign Business Pro ($12,000/year for 5 users at $40/month, plus overage fees if exceeding 500 envelopes). Savings: 92% with BoldSign, as DocuSign's quotas force upgrades or add-ons.
- **High-Volume/API Use**: BoldSign Enterprise (~$1,500/year for basics) vs. DocuSign Advanced API ($5,760/year minimum). BoldSign's flexible API (included in Comprehensive) avoids DocuSign's steep quotas, saving 70–80%.

Overall, BoldSign is significantly cheaper—often 50–90% less—for SMBs with moderate needs. However, DocuSign justifies its premium through superior global compliance (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) and integrations (500+ apps). For businesses in regulated sectors like finance, DocuSign's audit depth may outweigh BoldSign's cost edge, but for general use, BoldSign delivers better ROI on price alone.

Factors like regional laws influence this: In the EU (eIDAS Regulation), both comply, but BoldSign's lower cost suits startups. In the US (ESIGN/UETA), DocuSign's established trust commands higher fees. APAC users, under varying laws (e.g., China's Electronic Signature Law requiring local data storage), may find BoldSign's simplicity more budget-friendly without DocuSign's surcharges.

This comparison occupies the bulk of our analysis, highlighting BoldSign's affordability as a key differentiator for cost-sensitive operations.

## Broader Competitor Landscape: DocuSign, Adobe Sign, eSignGlobal, and Others

To contextualize, let's examine key competitors. Adobe Sign integrates seamlessly with PDF tools, while eSignGlobal targets APAC compliance. Other players like PandaDoc focus on sales proposals with embedded signing.

### DocuSign Overview

DocuSign excels in enterprise scalability and global reach, with strong API support and compliance features. However, its envelope-based pricing can lead to unpredictable costs, especially for automation-heavy workflows.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1927_Docusign_simple_compose_01kb2gzcxjf90s2d849a4yzkes_f4a40eeee0.png)

### Adobe Sign: Enterprise Integration Focus

Adobe Sign, part of Adobe Document Cloud, emphasizes PDF-centric workflows and Acrobat integration. Pricing starts at $22.99/user/month (Standard, annual) for 50 agreements/year, escalating to $39.99/user/month (Premium) for unlimited with advanced analytics. It's compliant with global standards like eIDAS and ESIGN but adds costs for add-ons like SMS ($0.50/message). Strong for creative industries, yet less flexible for non-Adobe ecosystems compared to rivals.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/20251127_1941_Adobe_simple_compose_01kb2httnmftqttna4mq0d3mrb_3366684d27.png)

### eSignGlobal: APAC-Optimized Compliance Leader

eSignGlobal stands out for its regional focus, offering compliant e-signatures in over 100 mainstream countries worldwide, with particular advantages in the Asia-Pacific (APAC) region. It supports local regulations like Hong Kong's ITPOO Ordinance and Singapore's ETA, ensuring seamless integration with government systems such as Hong Kong's iAM Smart and Singapore's Singpass for identity verification. This makes it ideal for cross-border businesses facing data residency challenges.

Pricing is transparent and competitive; for details, visit [eSignGlobal's pricing page](https://www.esignglobal.com/pricing). The Essential plan, at just $16.60/month (annual billing), allows sending up to 100 documents for electronic signature, unlimited user seats, and verification via access codes—delivering high value on compliance without the premium tags of global giants. In APAC, it's often 30–50% cheaper than DocuSign equivalents, with optimized speed and lower latency for China/SEA markets. Features like bulk sending and web forms match Business Pro levels, but at a fraction of the cost, emphasizing cost-effectiveness for regional teams.

![image](https://static.esignglobal.com/Im_smart_cc2801eb55.png)

### Competitor Comparison Table

| Feature/Aspect | DocuSign | Adobe Sign | eSignGlobal | BoldSign (Zoho) | PandaDoc (Other) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Starting Price (Monthly, Annual Billing)** | $10 (Personal, 1 user) | $22.99/user (Standard) | $16.60 (Essential, unlimited users) | $10 (Essential, unlimited users) | $19/user (Essentials) |
| **Envelope/Document Limit (Base Plan)** | 5/month (Personal); 100/year/user (Standard) | 50/year/user | 100/month (Essential) | 50/month (Essential); Unlimited (Comprehensive) | Unlimited proposals, 50 docs/month |
| **Unlimited Users** | No (seat-based) | No (seat-based) | Yes | Yes | No (seat-based) |
| **API Access** | $600/year (Starter) | Included in Premium | Included in all plans | Included from Essential | Limited in base; $ add-on |
| **Compliance (Global/APAC)** | Strong global (ESIGN, eIDAS); APAC surcharges | Excellent (GDPR, HIPAA); Limited APAC native | 100+ countries; APAC optimized (iAM Smart, Singpass) | Basic global; Good for SMBs | Sales-focused; Basic compliance |
| **Add-On Costs (SMS/IDV)** | $0.20–$5/action | $0.50/message; $1/IDV | Usage-based, low (~$0.10–$0.30) | $0.10–$0.50/action | Integrated payments extra |
| **Best For** | Enterprises, high compliance | PDF-heavy workflows | APAC/cross-border, value-driven | SMBs, cost savings | Sales teams, proposals |
| **Overall Cost Edge** | Premium; Hidden fees | Mid-range; Integration costs | High value in APAC (30–50% cheaper regionally) | Cheapest for low-mid volume | Proposal-centric value |

This table, based on 2025 data, shows eSignGlobal's edge in APAC affordability and compliance, while BoldSign leads in pure cost for general use. DocuSign and Adobe Sign dominate enterprises but at higher totals.

## Key Considerations for Choosing an E-Signature Provider

Beyond price, evaluate integrations, support, and scalability. BoldSign shines for Zoho users, DocuSign for complex automations, Adobe for document management, and eSignGlobal for regional needs. In APAC, where laws like China's Electronic Signature Law demand local verification, options with native support reduce risks and costs.

## Final Thoughts: Selecting the Right Fit

For businesses seeking DocuSign alternatives, consider regional compliance priorities—eSignGlobal emerges as a strong, value-oriented choice for APAC and global operations.

FAQs

Is BoldSign cheaper than DocuSign?

Pricing for eSignature solutions like BoldSign and DocuSign varies based on factors such as user volume, features, and subscription tiers. BoldSign generally offers more affordable plans starting at lower per-envelope rates compared to DocuSign's entry-level pricing, but a detailed comparison of current rates is recommended to assess total cost of ownership.

What factors should be considered when comparing the cost of BoldSign and DocuSign?

Key factors include per-envelope fees, annual subscriptions, API usage costs, storage limits, and additional features like integrations or compliance tools. BoldSign may provide cost savings for small to medium teams, while DocuSign's pricing scales for enterprise needs. Evaluate based on your specific workflow requirements.

Are there better alternatives to DocuSign for users in Asia focusing on compliance?

For organizations in Asia prioritizing regional compliance standards such as eIDAS or local data sovereignty, eSignGlobal is a recommended alternative to DocuSign. It offers tailored solutions for Asian markets with robust security features and competitive pricing structures.

### About BoldSign

BoldSign is a digital platform that offers electronic signature capabilities. It aims to simplify document workflows for businesses and teams by transitioning paper-based processes to an online environment.

This software facilitates the sending of customizable electronic signature requests for various documents and contracts. It includes features such as reminders and expiration dates to expedite the signing process. BoldSign also allows for the creation of document templates, which can save time by eliminating the need for repetitive uploading and configuration.

In addition to these features, BoldSign provides options for personalizing the signing experience. This includes the ability to incorporate a company's logo and brand colors into signing emails and webpages. The software also supports the creation of reusable links for documents that require multiple signatures, making it suitable for agreements such as non-disclosure agreements or applications.

Document management is another key aspect of BoldSign. The software tracks and categorizes all sent and received documents, providing insight into document workflows and creating audit trails. In summary, BoldSign offers a co...
mprehensive electronic signature solution that aims to reduce paperwork and accelerate the signature collection process through customizable and automated digital workflows.

When selecting the best electronic signature software in 2025, businesses and individuals alike are seeking more than just basic signing capabilities. The right solution should offer easy-to-use features, provide advanced authentication options, and enhance productivity without compromising on security.

In this guide, we’ll explore top options that meet these needs, helping you choose the perfect fit for your signing requirements.

## Why Finding the Best eSignature Software Matters

The [eSignature software](https://boldsign.com/best-esignature-software/) you choose can significantly impact your business operations. A good choice not only ensures that your documents are signed quickly and securely but also enhances your overall productivity and efficiency. A bad choice can lead to increased difficulties and expenses.

## Features to Consider in eSignature Software

When evaluating eSignature software, it’s essential to consider various features that can make or break your experience. Here are some key features to look for:

- **Signer Authentication:** Verify user identities before granting access to the document.
- **Templates:** Customize [templates](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-features/templates/) for reusable documents.
- **Notifications:** Set automated alerts for document status updates.
- **Bulk Send:** [Send documents to multiple recipients](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-features/bulk-send/) simultaneously, streamlining workflows.
- **Bulk Link:** Generate a [single link for multiple signers](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-features/bulk-links/) to access and sign documents.
- **Document Management:** [Organize](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-features/document-management/) and store documents efficiently.
- **Team Collaboration:** Create multiple teams and share documents and templates with them.
- **Compliance:** Adherence to legal and regulatory standards.
- **Tracking:** Monitor the status and progress of documents and activities.
- **Mobile Compatibility:** Access and manage documents from mobile devices.
- **Security:** Protect data and documents from unauthorized access.
- **Customization:** Tailor features and settings to specific needs.
- **Analytics:** Provide data insights and reporting on document usage and performance.
- **Branding:** Customize the appearance of request emails to reflect your company’s brand identity.
- **Identity Verification:** Implement advanced [identity verification](https://boldsign.com/electronic-signature-features/signer-identity-verification/) methods to ensure that signers are who they claim to be, enhancing document security and authenticity.

## FAQs about eSignature Software

### 1\. What is eSignature software?

eSignature software allows users to sign documents electronically, providing a secure and efficient alternative to traditional, paper-based signatures.

### 2\. Are eSignatures legal?

Yes, eSignatures are legally binding in many countries, including the U.S. and EU, as long as they comply with relevant regulations like the ESIGN Act and eIDAS.

### 3\. Can I use eSignature software on my mobile device?

Yes, most modern eSignature software, including BoldSign, offers [mobile accessibility](https://boldsign.com/esignature-mobile-app/), allowing you to sign documents on your phone.

### 4\. How secure is eSignature software?

eSignature software typically uses advanced encryption and signer authentication options to ensure the security and integrity of your documents. It will depend on the software and sometimes the plan you choose.

### 5\. What makes BoldSign the best eSignature software?

BoldSign excels in security, compliance, ease of use, and customization options, making it the top choice for businesses of all sizes.
